economic_notes: COVID-19 resulted in $4,408,575 in additional ISC funding for public health measures and Indigenous Community Support Fund. Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust fair market value $23,882,658. Loan guarantees for related parties totaling $7,440,985. Multiple limited partnerships and business enterprises operating in oilfield services, catering, fabrication, pipeline, land site services, real estate, retail, laundry, and industry relations.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to inability to determine impairment on investments held by Chipewyan Prairie Business Trust. Fiscal year 2021 statements were signed December 16, 2022 — significant delay in reporting. Three entities fully consolidated (Land Claim Settlement Trust, Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust, Community Trust). Sixteen entities accounted for by modified equity method including business trusts and controlled entities. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $7,750,117 + CMHC $261,065 + First Nation Development Funds $531,028 + Other government funding $221,945 + Athabasca Tribal Council $150,689 + Capital/Revenue Band Funds $7,904 = $8,922,748 (Ottawa Trust additions $7,905 also included bringing to ~$8,930,653; figure used is sum of identifiable government transfers). OSR calculated as total revenue minus total government transfers. COVID-19 resulted in $4,408,575 additional ISC funding included in the ISC total. Remuneration schedule data not available in provided text excerpt.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
